findings.evidence: The HTML contains a visually formatted version of the page content (including logos and layout) followed by a redundant, raw text-only flow of the exact same content.
The text content for this page, including headers and list items, appears twice in the HTML rendering, severely disrupting the reading order.
The text content for this page, including the header and paragraphs, appears twice sequentially in the HTML rendering.

findings.description: Page content is duplicated and fragmented.
findings.evidence: Text elements such as the header and footer information appear multiple times. The reading order is broken by duplicated text blocks surrounding an image block.

findings.description: Text paragraphs and signature rendered as a single image.
findings.evidence: The middle section of the page, containing several paragraphs of text ('As required by...' to 'Sincerely,') and the handwritten signature, is rendered as one large composite image rather than selectable text and a discrete signature image.
